reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Gregory Price agreed that the proposed change of moving daxdev initialization to iomap setup time is reasonable, noting it would save round trips and potentially solve performance issues.

Reviewer questioned the value of a metadata blob in struct inode, noting that if there was a generic format it would simply overload BPF storage
reviews.raw_body:
I think this is reasonable.  I'm not a FUSE wizard either, but I would
presume the iomap_setup() process would just essentially be John's
existing GET_FMAP / GET_DAXDEV code bundled.

GET_DAXDEV happens lazily to save him the round-trips to userland if the
DAXDEVs have already been seen previously.  I think your proposal does
in fact save him further round trips, and it would probably solve the
performance impact he saw from porting to FUSE.

---

Note that Christian Brauner just said he preferred not having dedicated
bpf storage in struct inode [1].

sans BPF, is there value in such a metadata blob existing?

If there was a generic format, then I suppose the blob storage would not
be BPF specific, it would just overload it (simple union).

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/20260421-arsch-gelernt-e0b5bcd8a7ff@brauner/T/#m8fea90f5ed4a1b23bdc2563d978948b263b2030b

~Gregory

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed Christian's concern about the blob residing within struct inode vs struct fuse_inode, agreed that it should not touch or add any infrastructure outside of fuse, and suggested a custom in-arena hash table as an alternative to bpf arenas.
reviews.raw_body:
I'm not sure if this addresses Christian's concerns or not, but the
blob would reside within struct fuse_inode not struct inode. I
definitely agree with him that this should not touch or add any infra
outside fuse.

I hadn't heard of bpf arenas until his comment. If the hashmap
overhead is too high for famfs, having a custom in-arena hash table
would be much faster I think, as it could be designed to require less
pointer chasing and avoid other overhead in the bpf hashmap
implementation, though now famfs would have to manage the data
structure and complexity itself.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Reviewer prefers using the fuse-inode blob instead of bpf-specific implementation, suggesting a generic format for the interface
reviews.raw_body:
I think if the fuse-inode blob is acceptable, that's highly preferable -
and it wouldn't necessarily need to stay specific to bpf.  If there was
some generic format that folks agreed on as a baseline, that could
obviously be worked into the interface.

~Gregory

2026-04-21.patch_summary: This patch series ports the FAMFS file system into the Fuse framework, removing the need for the GET_DAXDEV operation by integrating device initialization and setup with the iomap infrastructure. This change simplifies the kernel code, improves performance by reducing context switching costs, and aligns with Darrick's fuse-iomap work. The patch also discusses potential future directions, including using a generic BPF interface to handle both dax and non-dax file systems.
